steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims energy costs prevent full-speed use even during escape — the highest-stakes scenario. The graph data must show animals running below maximum speed when fleeing a predator.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "most lizard species use about the same percentage of their maximal speed when escaping predation as they do when pursuing prey.": shows similar speed percentages across contexts but doesn't isolate the energy-constraint explanation for escape specifically. ✗
  • "multiple lizard species move at an average of less than 90% of their maximal speed while escaping predation.": directly shows animals don't reach full speed even under predation pressure, which is exactly the evidence that energy costs constrain escape speed. ✓
  • "more lizard species use, on average, 90% -100% of their maximal speed while escaping predation than use any other percentage of their maximal speed.": suggests high-speed use predominates, contradicting the passage's claim. ✗
  • "at least 4 lizard species use, on average, less than 100% of their maximal speed while pursuing prey.": focuses on pursuit behavior rather than escape, missing the passage's specific claim. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
